name: Architext 1.7.3 Diagram Fidelity + Distribution (routing-model groundwork)
summary: Patch release. User-facing: sequence diagrams now follow the UML standard, the Sequence-mode flow-selection papercut and an overline-tracking drift are fixed, a Homebrew formula installs the native binary, and the serve plan farm warms incrementally so the first diagram appears fast. Under the hood, a from-scratch deterministic Rust routing model lands behind the ARCHITEXT_ROUTING_MODEL review flag (default routing is unchanged — still the engine): a locked shape law, a permanent forbidden-artifact gate, and crossings cut 28 -> 21, beating the engine on bend cost (beta 174 vs 1338) at equal crossings. That model is groundwork toward a future default switch, not a default change in 1.7.3.
updateSource: Shipped to npm 2026-06-21. Cut via develop -> main (#241) + just github-release; publish.yml fully green (release gate, publish binaries+launcher, attach binaries + SHA256SUMS, roboticus-site dispatch, published-CLI smoke all passed). Verified live against the registry: launcher @robotaccomplice/architext 1.7.3 with dist-tag latest=1.7.3, all five per-platform binary packages at 1.7.3, and the GitHub release carries the five binaries + SHA256SUMS. The deterministic router ships opt-in behind ARCHITEXT_ROUTING_MODEL; default rendering remains the engine, so this release carries no default routing change.

required.title: Deterministic routing model behind ARCHITEXT_ROUTING_MODEL (groundwork + gate)
required.summary: A from-scratch deterministic Rust router built and hardened over this cycle, exposed behind the ARCHITEXT_ROUTING_MODEL review flag — DEFAULT rendering is unchanged (still the engine). It carries a locked shape law (straight/L/C/Z/dogleg), shape-preserving channel separation (overlaps 8 -> 0; channel-buffer tight 19 -> 2), and a PERMANENT forbidden-artifact gate (zero doglegs, Z/staircases, non-orthogonal segments, unrouted edges, min-stems, or channel overlaps) enforced by the audit_model binary + an in-repo corpus test. Crossings fall 28 -> 21 (model == engine, never worse) while beating the engine on bend cost (beta 174 vs 1338); in model mode the engine's separate_close_parallel_routes is skipped and channel separation runs once, taking dense flows from minutes to milliseconds. Groundwork toward making the model the default in a later release.

required.title: Clear clippy -D warnings on the routing crate
required.summary: The wasm viewer clippy CI step (which lints architext-routing as a dependency under -D warnings) was red on lint debt accumulated across the routing build-out, uncaught because cargo test does not run clippy. Cleared (unused_mut, map_or, an OR-pattern range, two provably-dead route-cache assignments) with routing output byte-identical.

required.title: Sequence diagrams follow the UML standard
required.summary: Return messages are inferred by call/return pairing (untagged corpus returns now render dashed with an open arrowhead; synchronous calls stay solid with a filled head) and close their activation bars. Combined fragments render a SOLID border (dashing is reserved for return/async messages and lifelines), a pentagon operator tab, no adjacent-fragment overlap, and a right-aligned guard clear of the first message label.

required.title: Sequence-mode flow preservation + overline tracking
required.summary: Switching into Sequence mode no longer resets the flow dropdown to the first flow (it kept the selection's view but discarded the flow). The design-fidelity audit converged three overline labels to var(--overline-tracking) per DESIGN.md item 2; the audit was otherwise clean (role color single-sourced via --c4-*, state never a role hue, no rogue fonts).

required.title: Homebrew formula for the native binary
required.summary: A Homebrew formula installs the native architext binary, adding a first-class macOS/Linux distribution path alongside the curl installer and the npm bridge.

required.title: Incremental plan-farm warm — fast first diagram
required.summary: The serve plan farm warms incrementally so the first diagram renders quickly instead of waiting on the whole corpus to precompute.

deferred.title: Collapse the greedy mount-order passes into a direct track assignment
deferred.summary: Deferred after a measured prototype disproved the hypothesis (Rule 17): a global iterative-barycenter track assignment lands on the SAME 21 crossings the greedy finds (the residual is a structural optimum, not order-fixable), and the assignment ALONE (greedy disabled) gives 92 crossings, 4x worse. The greedy local search's per-candidate crossing evaluation is irreducible; a direct assignment merely re-derives it. Deferral reason: the refactor would not simplify or improve quality, so the current greedy stands.

deferred.title: Promote the deterministic routing model to the default
deferred.summary: The deterministic model ships behind ARCHITEXT_ROUTING_MODEL in 1.7.3 (review/groundwork). Making it the default requires a live-UI review pass across all flows and a decision on the residual 21 (structural) crossings vs the engine's tradeoffs. Deferral reason: sequencing — the model wants maintainer review in the live viewer before it replaces the engine for all users.

workstreams.name: Deterministic routing model (behind flag) + gate
workstreams.summary: Land the from-scratch deterministic router behind ARCHITEXT_ROUTING_MODEL with a locked shape law, channel separation, a permanent forbidden-artifact gate, and crossing reduction — verified against the FlowForge corpus, default routing untouched.

workstreams.name: Sequence + design fidelity
workstreams.summary: Sequence diagrams to the UML standard, a design-language fidelity pass over the viewer chrome, and the Sequence-mode flow-selection papercut.

workstreams.name: Distribution + serve performance
workstreams.summary: Add a Homebrew distribution path for the native binary and warm the serve plan farm incrementally so the first diagram appears fast.

evidence.label: PR #238 routing model + sequence/design fidelity + clippy (merged to develop)
PR #235 Homebrew formula for the native binary
PR #234 incremental plan-farm warm (fast first diagram)
